role_title: Product Manager, Structured Investments - Assistant Vice President / Vice President

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in product management
Title range: Assistant Vice President / Vice President
Base salary $130,000–$190,000 depending on level and experience
Desire to grow into broader product leadership responsibilities

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in product management, preferably within fintech, wealth management, capital markets, or related industries
must_haves.qualifier: preferably within fintech, wealth management, capital markets, or related industries

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated ability to independently drive complex initiatives and operate with a high degree of ownership and accountability
Willingness to become an expert in the structured investments space, understanding both the product intricacies and broader industry dynamics to drive informed decision-making and product leadership
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a proven track record of solving complex and unfamiliar problems through innovative solutions
Comfortable operating in fast-paced, evolving environments with changing priorities and imperfect information
Strong organizational and prioritization skills, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously while maintaining high-quality execution
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, including the ability to build trust and alignment across technical and business teams
Customer-centric approach, with experience engaging directly with clients and stakeholders to gather insights and feedback
Technical acumen and comfort working closely with engineering teams to solve problems and deliver scalable solutions
Experience with agile methodologies and tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Figma, analytics dashboards, Postman, and modern AI productivity tools
Detail-oriented, and able to write comprehensive user stories and acceptance criteria

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Desire to grow into broader product leadership responsibilities within a rapidly scaling organization

scope.surface: Structured investments platform (end-to-end: conception through launch and iteration)
scope.business_model: B2B fintech platform serving wealth management firms, financial advisors, issuers, and wholesalers
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform scale and efficiency in structured investments
Stakeholder alignment and execution quality across initiatives
core_responsibilities: Engage with wealth management firms, financial advisors, structured investments issuers, wholesalers, and internal stakeholders to identify market opportunities
Own and drive meaningful product domains or strategic initiatives across the structured investments platform
Define product requirements by writing detailed user stories and acceptance criteria for software engineers
Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of product feature and workflow development from conception to launch
Communicate effectively with business stakeholders to gather requirements, provide updates, and align product vision
Develop and maintain comprehensive project communication plans
Investigate bugs and identify tactical solutions to production issues impacting users
Monitor the performance of product features post-launch and continuously seek opportunities for improvement
Leverage AI-powered tools and workflows to improve productivity, analysis, communication quality, and overall execution effectiveness

compensation.equity_form: equity for all full-time employees (form not specified)

green_flags: Equity offered to all full-time employees
Generous benefits including 100% employer-paid dental, vision, telemedicine, and virtual mental health counseling
Clear product ownership scope described (structured investments platform end-to-end)
Hybrid model with only one remote day (Friday) — predictable in-office schedule
High visibility role with described growth path into product leadership
red_flags: No city explicitly named for the office location — candidates cannot confirm geography without additional research
Monday–Thursday in-office requirement eliminates fully remote candidates
No visa sponsorship language — status unclear
Wide salary band ($60K spread) may indicate significant level ambiguity between AVP and VP

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title range AVP/VP, years requirement, and comp band)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and stakeholder list)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no city named in JD body; left empty)
ambiguities: '5+ years of experience in product management, preferably within fintech, wealth management, capital markets' — 'preferably' qualifier makes industry experience soft, but the years requirement is hard; classified years as hard, industry as a contextual qualifier on the same item
No office city or geography stated anywhere in the JD body, making it impossible to confirm work location city
Role title spans two seniority bands (AVP and VP) with a $60K salary spread — level to be determined by experience; treated as a single role with a range
